# Philosophy & Political Theory Quarterly — Author Guidelines (Synthetic Fixture)

**Journal:** Philosophy & Political Theory Quarterly
**Publisher:** Cambridge University Press
**ISSN:** 1111-2222 (print), 3333-4444 (online)
**URL:** https://www.example-pptq.com

## Aims and Scope

Philosophy & Political Theory Quarterly publishes original research in
philosophy, political theory, social philosophy, ethics, and institutional
analysis. The journal welcomes theoretical essays, conceptual articles, and
critical analyses at the intersection of philosophy and political institutions.

Topics of particular interest include: democratic theory, platform governance,
digital ethics, institutional design, deliberative democracy, justice theory,
philosophy of technology, and social epistemology.

## Article Types

1. Research articles (6,000-10,000 words)
2. Theoretical essays (5,000-8,000 words)
3. Critical commentaries (3,000-5,000 words)
4. Book reviews (1,500-3,000 words)

## Submission Requirements

- **Language:** English only
- **Abstract:** 150-250 words
- **Keywords:** 4-6
- **Word count:** 5,000-10,000 words for full articles
- **Citation style:** Chicago 17th edition (author-date)
- **File format:** .docx or LaTeX

## Language Policy

The journal publishes exclusively in English. All submissions, including
abstracts, keywords, and supplementary materials, must be in English.

## Peer Review

- **Model:** Double-blind peer review
- **Timeline:** Initial decision within 10 weeks
- **Anonymization:** Remove all author-identifying information

## Ethics

Authors must declare conflicts of interest.
